article.body_preview:
DHAKA - Bangladesh Bank (BB) today announced 'cautiously accommodative' Monetary Policy Statement (MPS) for the second half of the current fiscal year 2022-23 (H2FY23).

"BB's monetary and credit programs for H2FY23 will pursue a cautiously accommodative policy stance to contain inflationary and exchange rate pressures, support desired economic growth, ensuring the necessary flow of funds to the economy's productive and employment generating activities," said BB Governor Abdur Rouf Talukder whil

llm_connections.evidence_quote: “dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure, protect foreign exchange reserves and control imported inflation”
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article discusses imports at the country-wide policy level, matching the feature’s definition of aggregate goods brought into Bangladesh.

verification.quote_best_match: dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure, protect foreign exchange reserves and control imported inflation,"
verification.judge_reasoning: The article explicitly says BB's monetary policy seeks to "dissuade imports of non-essential commodities to reduce the exchange rate pressure, protect foreign exchange reserves and control imported inflation," which closely matches the quoted evidence. This is a country-level policy discussion about Bangladesh's imports, so the scope matches.
